Deontological ethics emphasizes duty and moral rules, so the best response adheres to the *truth* (a duty in Kantian ethics) without excuses or evasions. Option D truthfully acknowledges the absence of school obligations while avoiding unnecessary justification. The other options either lie (A, B) or provide irrelevant personal details (C), which conflict with the principle of honesty as a moral duty.
